## Optimizer portion — strict reproduction checklist (Attention Is All You Need, §5.3)

The optimizer block from §5.3 of the Transformer paper reads verbatim:

> "We used the Adam optimizer with β₁ = 0.9, β₂ = 0.98 and ε = 10⁻⁹." [1]

| Field | Exact value used in the paper |
|---|---|
| Optimizer | Adam |
| β₁ | 0.9 |
| β₂ | 0.98 |
| ε (epsilon) | 10⁻⁹ |

### Strict-reproduction checklist items

1. **Optimizer family:** Adam, as specified in §5.3 of the Transformer paper. [1]
2. **β₁ = 0.9** — set this exactly; do not omit or substitute. [1]
3. **β₂ = 0.98** — set this exactly. The original Adam paper specifies good defaults of β₁ = 0.9, β₂ = 0.999 and ε = 10⁻⁸ [2], so the Transformer's β₂ = 0.98 is a deliberate deviation from the published Adam defaults. [1]
4. **ε = 10⁻⁹** — set this exactly. The original Adam paper specifies ε = 10⁻⁸ as a good default [2], so the Transformer's ε = 10⁻⁹ is a deliberate deviation from the published Adam defaults. [1]
5. These four fields are the only optimizer hyperparameters listed in §5.3. The LR-schedule formula and the warmup-step setting belong to the schedule block, not the optimizer block, and should be tracked separately. [1]

Only the four fields above constitute the optimizer portion of the checklist; the LR schedule is intentionally excluded from this block.

Sources
[1] Attention Is All You Need, 4 Why Self-Attention, p. 7
[2] ADAM: A METHOD FOR STOCHASTIC OPTIMIZATION, 1 INTRODUCTION, p. 2
